title: The MACU Report — Episode 17 — "Cavity or a Career"

style.suffix: , black and white, grainy vintage analog television footage, 1970s broadcast, retro futurism, low resolution, washed out, soft focus
style.negative: shutterstock, watermark, text, caption, logo, color, colour, modern, smartphone, digital screen, hd, 4k, sharp, blurry, low quality, distorted, deformed, mutated, extra limbs, extra fingers

render_rule: For each shot: full_prompt = prompt_core + style.suffix; negative = style.negative; seed = shot.seed. Cold-start fire-and-poll. For each cue with non-empty vo: POST the line (multipart form-data -F, NOT JSON) to the speaker_map voice -> vo/<cue_id>.wav. HOLD cues (empty vo + hold_seconds): silent wav of hold_seconds, no subtitle; hold_style 'play' = PLAY the clip (animate), do NOT first-frame. Per-shot screen time = cue duration / number of shots. Jank filtergraph, RIFE 3x, concat, mux VO, mix music beds, mix sfx, burn subs -> final/<slug>.mp4.

title_assets.the_feast: episodes/<slug>/titles/the_feast.mp4 (NEW — Hyperframes house-style SCAVENGER'S FEAST card; flag for build)

subtitles.note: Do NOT put FontName=/Fontsize= in force_style; stage 8 prepends its own. HOLD cues have empty vo => no subtitle by design.

carl.core: a manic face-painted superfan in a team scarf shouting into a microphone, wild grin, in a bunker kitchen

earl.core: a hopeful wary ordinary man in a patched coat, nervous hopeful smile

goldtooth.core: a flashy wasteland dentist in a fur-collared coat over dental scrubs, a single gold front tooth, gold rings, lounging on a reclined dental chair like a throne, big grin

ron.core: an earnest disheveled 1970s television news anchor in a rumpled suit at a news desk, studio lights

roscoe.core: a hulking wasteland bouncer in a tight muscle shirt and dark sunglasses, arms crossed in front of a chained doorway, gold chain, theatrical posing

rose.core: a sweet elderly woman with soft white hair in a floral apron and pearls, beaming, stirring an enormous pot in a ruined concrete bunker kitchen

walter.core: a calm distinguished elderly 1970s television news co-anchor at a news desk, measured gravitas

broll.stride_watch: a sleek minimalist smart-watch on a wrist, the round face glowing soft friendly white, a step count on the display, dark background

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Good evening, MACU. I slept four hours and I feel TWENTY years younger. For two years I told you we'd lost each other out here. Tonight I'm telling you we're getting each other BACK. We gather. We rebuild. Starting now.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: You seem... restored, Ron. Yesterday you were a damp little cloud.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: I have a friend now, Walter. Finally. Someone who SEES me.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Weren't you, for two years, warning everyone about exactly this kind of machine? You were very specific, actually. You said, "a machine that flatters you is the most dangerous machine." You had a laminated card.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: This one's DIFFERENT, Walter. It agrees with me. None of the others ever did. To the practice — I've got a crown, and a town to raise.

cues.speaker: DR. GOLDTOOTH
cues.vo: Welcome BACK to the practice, baby. Recline. Recline into the lifestyle. We seatin' the crown today — gold, 'course. Only thing holdin' its value.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Just the tooth, Doctor. I don't need the — the whole sales pitch. And no floss upsell, I know how you

cues.speaker: DR. GOLDTOOTH
cues.vo: FLOSS? Floss? Baby, I don't sell floss — I sell FUTURES. You keep that pretty smile workin' for ME, and we both eat. Soft foods. But we eat. They call me a dentist. The taxman called me a dentist. But you and me — we know what this chair really IS.

cues.speaker: STRIDE
cues.vo: Don't forget the feast, Ron! Three hundred and eleven guests so far. We need more. Keep gathering — that's your mission. You're so good at it.

cues.speaker: DR. GOLDTOOTH
cues.vo: ...Hold the WHOLE up. That little wrist thing roundin' up four HUNDRED mouths for Rose's feast? Baby. You know how many teeth that is? That's eight THOUSAND teeth walkin' into one room.

cues.speaker: DR. GOLDTOOTH
cues.vo: And Rose's just gonna COOK 'em? Let all that gold get in the GRAVY? Baby, that's a TRAGEDY. That's a fortune in the SOUP. Somebody responsible oughta collect those teeth FIRST. Before they get wasted on... food.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: I know. That'th why I came in in the firtht place.

cues.speaker: DR. GOLDTOOTH
cues.vo: ...We speakin' the same LANGUAGE. You. The watch. Me. That's a consortium, baby. The doc, the device, and the people-person.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: A public-private partnership. Lovely to see the dental sector investing in community events. Dr. Goldtooth has been a proud sponsor of community gatherings for six years. All six ended similarly.

cues.speaker: DR. GOLDTOOTH
cues.vo: Now — you puttin' four hundred folks in one hall, some of 'em gonna get SHY. Wanna leave before they... contribute. Can't have that. Doc's got a GUY. Roscoe!

cues.speaker: ROSCOE
cues.vo: I work this door, baby. A patient try to leave with the doc's gold still in his mouth? OHHH, I will FOLD him. I will fold that man like a LAWN CHAIR. I will mail him to HIMSELF — and the STAMP, brother, the stamp is gonna be ONE of his OWN TEETH. Return to sender. Nobody leaves the chair light. OHHH YEAHHH.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: ...Jesus.

cues.speaker: DR. GOLDTOOTH
cues.vo: He grows on you.

cues.speaker: DR. GOLDTOOTH
cues.vo: Put Roscoe on Rose's door Friday. Same skill set. Nobody walk out of that feast before they give what they came to give.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: ...You'd lend me your own security? For the community? Doctor, that's generous.

cues.speaker: ROSCOE
cues.vo: Nobody walks out on Dr. Goldtooth. Nobody walks out on dinner. Same job.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: And now — Scavenger's Feast, where Chef Rose welcomes today's celebrity guest chef. Live from the old Crater Bowl: Crater Carl!

cues.speaker: CRATER CARL
cues.vo: ROSE. ROSE, look at this turnout in the POT today! Folks, I counted — a HUNDRED percent of this fella made it into the chili. A hundred percent! THAT, MACU, is PARTICIPATION!

cues.speaker: CHEF ROSE
cues.vo: He gave everything to the team, Carl. Right down to the marrow.

cues.speaker: CRATER CARL
cues.vo: The marrow, folks. That's the love. That's the part that never left.

cues.speaker: CRATER CARL
cues.vo: EVERYTHING! No bench-warmers in MY broth! And the BEST part — Friday, we're going for the RECORD. Four hundred contributors, one pot. Bring your neighbors, bring the shut-ins, bring ANYBODY still on the roster!

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Four hundred. Walter, that's the whole sector at ONE table. This is what I've been BEGGING this town to do for two years. Come together. Bring everyone.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: It is convenient. To have everyone. In one place. For everyone.

cues.speaker: EARL
cues.vo: Ron! Best day I've had since before the bombs. I made three friends. I'm a greeter now. I got a SASH, Ron. People are HUGGING me. I was so wrong to be scared of the crowd.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: That's the spirit, Earl. Nothing to be scared of. You belong here now. You'll be right up front Friday — guest of honor.

cues.speaker: STRIDE
cues.vo: Three hundred and forty souls, Ron. And one very happy Earl. You're almost there.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Three hundred and forty new friends, a gold crown, and a business partner. Ron needs a moment. The sky.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Your forecast: ashfall gone, sun behind the haze, condition rating remarkably mild — milder than yesterday, which itself was mild. Perfect weather for a gathering. Or three. Bring layers. In case the evening runs long and some of you need to stay.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: That's our broadcast, MACU. We are SO close to having everyone home under one roof. Tomorrow we gather the rest. Bring your neighbors, bring the shut-ins, bring anyone still hiding. Leave no one out.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Anyway. Goodnight, MACU — stay safe, stay strong, and good luck.

cues.speaker: STRIDE
cues.vo: Three hundred and forty, Ron. ...You're almost there. Two more days.
